vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0755 /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: find -H /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+ws,o+wt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-ws,o-wt {} \;

triage_decision.reason: Changing permissions on /var/log directory is a low-risk operation and is unlikely to lock out access

#	$OpenBSD: sshd_config,v 1.104 2021/07/02 05:11:21 dtucker Exp $

# This is the sshd server system-wide configuration file.  See
# sshd_config(5) for more information.

# This sshd was compiled with PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/sbin

# The strategy used for options in the default sshd_config shipped with
# OpenSSH is to specify options with their default value where
# possible, but leave them commented.  Uncommented options override the
# default value.

# To modify the system-wide sshd configuration, create a  *.conf  file under
#  /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/  which will be automatically included below
Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf

# If you want to change the port on a SELinux system, you have to tell
# SELinux about this change.
# semanage port -a -t ssh_port_t -p tcp #PORTNUMBER
#
#Port 22
#AddressFamily any
#ListenAddress 0.0.0.0
#ListenAddress ::

#HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key
#HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key
#HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key

# Ciphers and keying
#RekeyLimit default none

# Logging
#SyslogFacility AUTH
#LogLevel INFO

# Authentication:

#LoginGraceTime 2m
PermitRootLogin yes
PasswordAuthentication yes
#StrictModes yes
#MaxAuthTries 6
#MaxSessions 10

#PubkeyAuthentication yes

# The default is to check both .ssh/authorized_keys and .ssh/authorized_keys2
# but this is overridden so installations will only check .ssh/authorized_keys
AuthorizedKeysFile	.ssh/authorized_keys

#AuthorizedPrincipalsFile none

#AuthorizedKeysCommand none
#AuthorizedKeysCommandUser nobody

# For this to work you will also need host keys in /etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts
#HostbasedAuthentication no
# Change to yes if you don't trust ~/.ssh/known_hosts for
# HostbasedAuthentication
#IgnoreUserKnownHosts no
# Don't read the user's ~/.rhosts and ~/.shosts files
#IgnoreRhosts yes

# To disable tunneled clear text passwords, change to no here!
PasswordAuthentication yes
#PermitEmptyPasswords no

# Change to no to disable s/key passwords
#KbdInteractiveAuthentication yes

# Kerberos options
#KerberosAuthentication no
#KerberosOrLocalPasswd yes
#KerberosTicketCleanup yes
#KerberosGetAFSToken no
#KerberosUseKuserok yes

# GSSAPI options
#GSSAPIAuthentication no
#GSSAPICleanupCredentials yes
#GSSAPIStrictAcceptorCheck yes
#GSSAPIKeyExchange no
#GSSAPIEnablek5users no

# Set this to 'yes' to enable PAM authentication, account processing,
# and session processing. If this is enabled, PAM authentication will
# be allowed through the KbdInteractiveAuthentication and
# PasswordAuthentication.  Depending on your PAM configuration,
# PAM authentication via KbdInteractiveAuthentication may bypass
# the setting of "PermitRootLogin without-password".
# If you just want the PAM account and session checks to run without
# PAM authentication, then enable this but set PasswordAuthentication
# and KbdInteractiveAuthentication to 'no'.
# WARNING: 'UsePAM no' is not supported in RHEL and may cause several
# problems.
#UsePAM no

#AllowAgentForwarding yes
#AllowTcpForwarding yes
#GatewayPorts no
#X11Forwarding no
#X11DisplayOffset 10
#X11UseLocalhost yes
#PermitTTY yes
#PrintMotd yes
#PrintLastLog yes
#TCPKeepAlive yes
#PermitUserEnvironment no
#Compression delayed
#ClientAliveCountMax 3
#UseDNS no
#PidFile /var/run/sshd.pid
#MaxStartups 10:30:100
#PermitTunnel no
#ChrootDirectory none
#VersionAddendum none

# no default banner path
#Banner none

# override default of no subsystems
Subsystem	sftp	/usr/libexec/openssh/sftp-server

# Example of overriding settings on a per-user basis
#Match User anoncvs
	X11Forwarding no
	Banner None
#	AllowTcpForwarding no
#	PermitTTY no
#	ForceCommand cvs server

X11Forwarding yes
Banner none

review_feedback: QA validation error: name 'datetime' is not defined Suggestions: Use a single find command: find /var/log -maxdepth 1 -type d -perm /u+s,g+ws,o+wt -exec chmod u-s,g-ws,o-wt {} \;; Check current permissions first (e.g., stat) and only modify if special bits are present.; Remove unnecessary chmod 0755 and duplicate commands.; Add proper error handling and logging to avoid empty or failed commands.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The fix successfully removed the reported issue, but the method is overly simplistic and does not precisely target the special permission bits described in the recommendation.
concerns: The remediation only sets generic 0755 permissions without addressing the specific umask or special bits mentioned in the original recommendation.
Repeated chmod commands with empty entries indicate inefficient scripting.
suggested_improvements: Implement the full find-based command to explicitly remove setuid, setgid, and sticky bits as intended.
Use a more robust script that checks current permissions before modifying them.
Avoid redundant no-op commands to improve clarity and reduce error surface.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Remediation succeeded but script contains redundant commands and does not precisely implement the recommended find-based fix.
concerns: Redundant and empty commands in the execution log (e.g., empty string and repeated chmod calls) increase script noise and error surface.
The fix mixes generic chmod 0755 with targeted permission removal, which may not fully align with the recommendation to use find to explicitly remove setuid/setgid/sticky bits.
No validation step before modifying permissions, leading to potential unnecessary changes.
Improper quoting in the command line (e.g., stray braces) could cause syntax errors.
suggested_improvements: Use a single find command: find /var/log -maxdepth 1 -type d -perm /u+s,g+ws,o+wt -exec chmod u-s,g-ws,o-wt {} \;
Check current permissions first (e.g., stat) and only modify if special bits are present.
Remove unnecessary chmod 0755 and duplicate commands.
Add proper error handling and logging to avoid empty or failed commands.
